title: Ultra-Rapid Sequencing (LAMP)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"esj75","text":"This protocol accompanies the paper \"Ultra-Rapid Somatic Variant Detection via Real-Time Threshold Sequencing.\" This protocol was followed to initiate a sequencing run that resulted in a somatic variant call from known tumor tissue in less than 30 minutes. The protocol outlines DNA extraction, LAMP, library preparation for Oxford Nanopore Sequencing, and sequencer preparation and loading.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the LAMP reaction tube by mixing the following components in a 0.2 mL PCR tube 12.5 µL WarmStart LAMP Kit (DNA and RNA) - 500 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E1700L 2.5 µL 10 micromolar (µM) LAMP primer mix 9 µL Nuclease-free Water Contributed by users
Prepare the DNA extraction tube by mixing the following components in a 0.2 mL PCR tube 100 µL of Epicentre QuickExtract™ DNA Extraction Solution Epicentre Catalog #QE09050
When acquired, place 20 µg tumor tissue into DNA extraction tube
Vortex on high for 00:00:30
Place in pre-heated thermocycler and unpause combined program
Let thermocycler run through the DNA extraction protocol
Either let thermocycler draw DNA down to room temperature or quench in ice for 00:00:10 . Pause thermocycler program to pre-heat for LAMP. Briefly vortex on high and spin down.
Add 1 µL extracted DNA to LAMP reaction tube, being careful to avoid leftover tissue or other debris in tube.
Place in thermocycler and unpause combined protocol
During LAMP, prepare ONT tagmentation mix by mixing the following in a 0.2 mL PCR tube 2.5 µL FRA Buffer Oxford Nanopore Technologies 5.6 µL nuclease free water Contributed by users
Let thermocycler run through the LAMP protocol
Either let thermocycler draw DNA down to room temperature or quench in ice water for 00:00:10 . Pause thermocycler program to pre-heat for ONT rapid library preparation tagmentation.
Add 1.9 µL LAMP product to tagmentation mix, flick to mix, and spin down
Place in thermocycler and unpause combined protocol
Either let thermocycler draw DNA down to room temperature or quench in ice water for 00:00:10
Add 1 µL RAP (Rapid Adapter) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-RAD004 to tagmentation tube, flick to mix, and spin down
Incubate sequencing library for 00:02:00 at room temperature, flicking to mix occasionally and spinning down
During the final 1m of incubation, re-prime the ONT flow-cell with SpotOn port open, using 200 µL of FB (Flush Buffer) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-FLP002 mix
Add the entire 11ul library to the sequencing mix tube
Pipette entire 75 µL sequencing mix into the MinION Spot On port, by slowly squeezing droplets onto the ramp/port.
Unpause sequencing run in MinKNOW software
Let thermocycler run through the tagmentation protocol

Prepare ONT sequencing buffer 34 µL SQB (Sequencing Buffer) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-RAD004 SQB 25.5 µL LB (Loading Beads) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-RAD004 4.5 µL nuclease free water Contributed by users

Prepare ONT loading buffer Add 30 µL FLT (Flush Tether) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-FLP002 into tube of FB (Flush Buffer) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-FLP002 Mix and spin down

Pre-heat thermocycler initiate, then pause, combined Ultra-Rapid Program DNA Extraction 65 °C for 00:01:00 98 °C for 00:02:00 hold at 20 °C LAMP 65 °C for 00:14:00 Fragmentation 30 °C for 00:01:00 80 °C for 00:01:00 hold at 20 °C
Prepare ONT fragmentation mix 2.5 µL FRA (Fragmentation Mix) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-RAD004 5.6 µL nuclease free water Contributed by users
Prime flow cell with 800 µL of loading buffer and let incubate for 00:05:00
Start sequencing run in MinKNOW software and pause run immediately after flow cell QC check
creator: Jack Wadden
